content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Check-Triggered Prompting: Dynamic Injection of Retrieval for Factual Consistency in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently generate hallucinated or unsupported facts, undermining their reliability for factual tasks. Existing prompting methods are insufficient for dynamically steering models toward verifiable content.",
  "motivation": "Hallucinations remain a key limitation for LLM adoption in high-stakes domains such as healthcare, law, and education. Recent advances in LLM reasoning show promise, but static prompts fail to adapt to the real-time uncertainty of model generations. There is an urgent need for prompting approaches that can responsively guide LLMs toward factual content during generation.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting method that dynamically augments LLM outputs with fact-check triggers. The prompt includes an instruction for the model: 'If unsure, output [CHECK] and pause.' The output is parsed; upon encountering [CHECK], a retrieval module fetches relevant evidence snippets from a knowledge base, which are inserted into a follow-up prompt asking the LLM to regenerate or support its answer. This loop continues until the model produces a factually supported answer or abstains. The method leverages LLMs’ uncertainty signals to conditionally inject retrieval augmentation only when needed.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on fact-based QA datasets such as TriviaQA and HotpotQA. Baselines include vanilla prompting, chain-of-thought, and retrieval-augmented generation. Metrics will be factual accuracy (verified by humans and automated tools), hallucination rate, and answer completeness. Ablations will test variants (e.g., different uncertainty triggers, retrieval sources)."
}
